title: Kaboom Crew — offline solo vertical slice (MVP 0)
summary: Flagship dogfood sample for AGF: a top-down arcade chaos game with grid-based bombs, blocks, pickups and bots. MVP 0 is the offline single-player vertical slice — one sector, one bot, one win condition (clear all soft blocks or destroy the bot). Public name is Kaboom Crew; the internal design doc at notes/DynaBomber.md uses the codename DynaBomber. MVP 0 must run on the existing AGF stack with no networking, no procedural-character generator and no audio system; it exists to stress-test the agent loop against actual gameplay code.
doneCriteria: Damped follow camera + orthographic camera path shipped as engine primitives (ECS systems, not project-local helpers).
2D HUD / minimap runtime shipped as an engine primitive (DOM-backed v0 is acceptable).
Procedural-generator framework: seed → JSON scene + validation; one Kaboom Crew level template.
Bot AI primitives: danger map + basic pathfinding + one personality.
Bomb pipeline (project-local): fuse, blast propagation, chain reactions, block destruction, pickup spawn.
Bomberstats + power-up effects: Bomb-up, Fire-up, Speed-up (project-local).
Solo round terminates with a win or loss screen.
engine doctor passes; `examples/kaboom-crew/playtests/<scenario>.json` records a bot win.
Kaboom Crew is a public-name candidate — final trademark / uniqueness check happens at MVP 3, not before.
notes: Public name decided 2026-05-18. See [[project-flagship-sample-kaboom-crew]] memory entry.
Gap list and order-of-operations live at notes/dynabomber-readiness-analysis.md.
Engine pre-game work (camera / HUD / grid / generator / bot AI) lands in S081–S082 before any bomb code; project-local game systems land in `examples/kaboom-crew/` from S083 onward.
Multiplayer authority, client prediction, snapshot relevance live in a sibling KABOOM-CREW-MVP-1 epic (post-MVP-0).
